user.title: Connect your Comfortzone heat pump
user.description:
This integration uses the **Loggamera** cloud API to read and control your heat pump.

**What you need before continuing:**

1. 🔑 **API key & Device ID** — Log in to the [Loggamera portal](https://portal.loggamera.se), navigate to **Settings -> API** ([https://portal.loggamera.se/ApiSettings/Index/](https://portal.loggamera.se/ApiSettings/Index/)) to find both your API key and the numeric Device ID for your heat pump.
2. 🏷️ **Model** — Pick `RX95` for a Comfortzone RX95 (or `Other`). You can change this later.
3. 💸 **(Optional) Electricity price entity** — Pick a Nord Pool / electricity price sensor to enable per-mode cost tracking.

The integration polls every minute. The first refresh after setup may take a few seconds because the Loggamera API is sometimes slow.

data.price_in_ore: Price entity reports öre/kWh (uncheck for SEK/kWh)

data_description.api_key: Found under Settings -> API in your Loggamera account.
data_description.device_id: The numeric ID of your heat pump, found under Settings -> API.
data_description.model: Used for branding and future model-specific behavior.
data_description.price_entity: Examples: sensor.nord_pool_se3_aktuellt_pris (modern Nord Pool integration → SEK/kWh) or older Nord Pool sensors that still report öre/kWh.
data_description.price_in_ore: Modern Nord Pool already reports SEK/kWh — leave this off. Older or templated sensors may still report öre/kWh; tick the box in that case.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the Loggamera API. Check your internet connection and that portal.loggamera.se is online, then try again.
error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed. Double-check the API key and Device ID — both must belong to the same Loggamera account.
error.unknown: Something unexpected happened during validation. See Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: A Comfortzone heat pump with this Device ID is already configured.

init.title: Comfortzone heat pump options
init.description: Adjust integration settings. Changes reload the integration automatically.

data.price_in_ore: Price entity reports öre/kWh (uncheck for SEK/kWh)
data.compressor_electrical_factor: Compressor factor override (0 = use spec curve)
data.low_hw_threshold_c: Low hot water alarm — trip temperature (°C)
data.large_draw_threshold_c: Large hot water draw — sensitivity (°C of missing heat)

data_description.price_entity: Pick the entity that reports your current electricity spot price. Required for the heating cost / hot water cost sensors to update.
data_description.price_in_ore: Modern Nord Pool sensors report SEK/kWh — leave this off. Tick if your sensor reports öre/kWh.
data_description.compressor_electrical_factor: Leave at 0 to use the selected model's EN255 spec curve (RX95: factor 0.235 at 35°C flow → 0.314 at 50°C flow). Models without a known curve fall back to a generic 0.30 factor. Set a non-zero value (e.g. 0.4) to override with a constant thermal-to-electrical conversion.
data_description.short_cycle_threshold: Default 6. Trips the short-cycling alarm when the compressor starts more than this many times in a 1-hour rolling window.
data_description.addition_power_threshold_w: Default 500 W. The addition heater alarm only counts samples that draw at least this much power.
data_description.addition_duration_threshold_s: Default 600 s (10 min). Sustained addition-heater operation must exceed this duration before the alarm trips.
data_description.filter_warning_days: Default 7. The soft filter warning trips when fewer than this many days remain on the filter timer.
data_description.low_hw_threshold_c: Default 40 °C. The low-hot-water alarm trips when the tank drops below this temperature.
data_description.low_hw_hysteresis_c: Default 3 °C. The alarm clears when the tank exceeds (threshold + hysteresis), preventing on/off bouncing.
data_description.max_load_threshold_pct: Default 90 %. The compressor-running-at-max signal trips when the inverter frequency reaches this share of its maximum.
data_description.max_load_duration_s: Default 300 s (5 min). The signal only trips after the load has been continuously above the threshold for at least this duration.
data_description.large_draw_threshold_c: Default 3 °C. How much the tank must fall short of its expected temperature (integrated over time) before the 'Large hot water draw' sensor turns on. Lower = more sensitive (catches smaller draws but more false positives), higher = only big draws like showers and baths.
data_description.long_hw_cycle_min: Default 50 min. A completed hot-water production cycle longer than this is flagged 'unusually long' on the cycle-duration sensor, hinting that someone drew hot water while the pump was already producing.
